findings.title: Reversed logic for overriding source manifest when input is supplied
findings.summary: The intakeResolver incorrectly discards the existing manifest when the user does NOT supply input (!inputResolution.supplied), instead of when the user DOES supply input.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/intakeResolver.ts:59 - `if (!inputResolution.supplied && manifest && inputResolution.existing.length > 0)` incorrectly checks `!inputResolution.supplied` to blow away `manifest`. If the user supplies an explicit input, the manifest should be recreated, but if they rely on defaults, the existing manifest should be preserved.

findings.title: Uncommitted worktree changes are ignored and destroyed during merge
findings.summary: mergeWorktree merges the branch but fails to commit the unstaged working tree changes made by the worker first. Uncommitted changes are silently dropped when the worktree is deleted.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/worktreeIsolation.ts:63 - `git diff --stat HEAD branchName` only compares commits. Since the worker edited files but did not commit them, the branch has no commits.
packages/remediate-code/src/steps/worktreeIsolation.ts:76 - `git merge --no-ff branchName` is run, which sees the branch is an ancestor of HEAD and merges nothing.
packages/remediate-code/src/steps/worktreeIsolation.ts:80 - `removeWorktree` is called, which force-deletes the worktree, discarding all uncommitted changes.
